title: Kingdom, Psalms, Prophets, Exile, and Return
unitGoal: Students practise the kingdom-to-return timeline and learn how kings, psalms, prophets, exile, and return point toward Christ.

lessons.title: Judges and the Need for a King
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the cycle of sin, oppression, crying out, rescue, and the need for faithful leadership.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the cycle of sin, oppression, crying out, rescue, and the need for faithful leadership.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Judges and the Need for a King Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand why Israel needed faithful rule under God.

steps.title: Judges Pattern
steps.prompt: The book of Judges often shows a cycle of...
choices: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
short-term rescue followed by lasting faithfulness
prophets, temple worship, and rebuilding
steps.correctAnswer: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
steps.feedback: Correct. Israel repeatedly turned from God and needed rescue.
steps.hint: Israel repeatedly turned from God and needed rescue.

steps.title: False Worship
steps.prompt: Israel's worship of other gods showed...
choices: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
a practical way to fit in with nearby cultures
a small compromise to keep peace with neighbours
steps.correctAnswer: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
steps.feedback: Correct. Idolatry broke covenant faithfulness.
steps.hint: Idolatry broke covenant faithfulness.

steps.title: God's Mercy
steps.prompt: God raised up judges as acts of mercy toward His oppressed people.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. The judges were imperfect rescuers, but God's mercy was real.
steps.hint: The judges were imperfect rescuers, but God's mercy was real.

steps.title: No King
steps.prompt: The repeated phrase about no king in Israel points to...
choices: the need for faithful rule under God
strong local leaders could solve the heart problem
a time when family loyalty mattered more than covenant faithfulness
steps.correctAnswer: the need for faithful rule under God
steps.feedback: Correct. Judges shows the disorder of doing what seems right apart from God.
steps.hint: Judges shows the disorder of doing what seems right apart from God.

steps.title: Imperfect Rescuers
steps.prompt: The judges were...
choices: real rescuers but not the final answer to sin
temporary rescuers who could change hearts
national leaders with the same role as David
steps.correctAnswer: real rescuers but not the final answer to sin
steps.feedback: Correct. They helped Israel but could not fix the deeper problem.
steps.hint: They helped Israel but could not fix the deeper problem.

steps.title: A Better King
steps.prompt: Judges helps readers long for...
choices: a righteous king who leads God's people faithfully
stable leadership without repentance
a stronger government more than a faithful king
steps.correctAnswer: a righteous king who leads God's people faithfully
steps.feedback: Correct. The book points forward to the need for faithful kingship.
steps.hint: The book points forward to the need for faithful kingship.

quiz.title: Judges and the Need for a King Quiz

questions.prompt: The book of Judges often shows a cycle of...
choices: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
short-term rescue followed by lasting faithfulness
prophets, temple worship, and rebuilding
questions.correctAnswer: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
questions.reviewNote: Israel repeatedly turned from God and needed rescue.

questions.prompt: Israel's worship of other gods showed...
choices: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
a practical way to fit in with nearby cultures
a small compromise to keep peace with neighbours
questions.correctAnswer: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
questions.reviewNote: Idolatry broke covenant faithfulness.

questions.prompt: God raised up judges as acts of mercy toward His oppressed people.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: The judges were imperfect rescuers, but God's mercy was real.

questions.prompt: The repeated phrase about no king in Israel points to...
choices: the need for faithful rule under God
strong local leaders could solve the heart problem
a time when family loyalty mattered more than covenant faithfulness
questions.correctAnswer: the need for faithful rule under God
questions.reviewNote: Judges shows the disorder of doing what seems right apart from God.

questions.prompt: The judges were...
choices: real rescuers but not the final answer to sin
temporary rescuers who could change hearts
national leaders with the same role as David
questions.correctAnswer: real rescuers but not the final answer to sin
questions.reviewNote: They helped Israel but could not fix the deeper problem.

lessons.title: Samuel, Saul, and David
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise God's word through Samuel, Saul's disobedience, David's heart, and the covenant promise.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise God's word through Samuel, Saul's disobedience, David's heart, and the covenant promise.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Samuel, Saul, and David Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand why God rejected Saul and made covenant promises to David.

steps.title: Hearing God's Word
steps.prompt: Samuel's call showed that Israel needed...
choices: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
leaders who kept peace by softening hard messages
a prophet who spoke only when people agreed
steps.correctAnswer: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
steps.feedback: Correct. Samuel learned to listen and speak God's word.
steps.hint: Samuel learned to listen and speak God's word.

steps.title: Saul's Failure
steps.prompt: Saul's failure showed that God desires...
choices: obedience more than outward religious action
public worship that covers private rebellion
leaders who protect reputation before obedience
steps.correctAnswer: obedience more than outward religious action
steps.feedback: Correct. Samuel told Saul that obedience is better than sacrifice.
steps.hint: Samuel told Saul that obedience is better than sacrifice.

steps.title: Obedience
steps.prompt: Partial obedience is not the same as faithful obedience.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Saul tried to explain disobedience as if it were worship.
steps.hint: Saul tried to explain disobedience as if it were worship.

steps.title: The Heart
steps.prompt: When God chose David, He showed that He sees...
choices: the heart, not only outward appearance
outward ability as the safest way to choose
appearance and experience above the heart
steps.correctAnswer: the heart, not only outward appearance
steps.feedback: Correct. God told Samuel that He looks on the heart.
steps.hint: God told Samuel that He looks on the heart.

steps.title: Covenant Promise
steps.prompt: God promised David...
choices: a kingdom and throne connected to his family line
that David's family would never need correction
that a king could remove the need for repentance
steps.correctAnswer: a kingdom and throne connected to his family line
steps.feedback: Correct. The Davidic covenant points forward to Christ.
steps.hint: The Davidic covenant points forward to Christ.

steps.title: David's Sin and Mercy
steps.prompt: David was a true king, but his later sin shows...
choices: even chosen leaders need mercy and repentance
God's promise would be carried by ordinary human strength
leaders can keep serving without honest repentance
steps.correctAnswer: even chosen leaders need mercy and repentance
steps.feedback: Correct. David's failures do not cancel God's promise, but they show the need for a greater King.
steps.hint: David's failures do not cancel God's promise, but they show the need for a greater King.

questions.prompt: Samuel's call showed that Israel needed...
choices: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
leaders who kept peace by softening hard messages
a prophet who spoke only when people agreed
questions.correctAnswer: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
questions.reviewNote: Samuel learned to listen and speak God's word.

questions.prompt: Saul's failure showed that God desires...
choices: obedience more than outward religious action
public worship that covers private rebellion
leaders who protect reputation before obedience
questions.correctAnswer: obedience more than outward religious action
questions.reviewNote: Samuel told Saul that obedience is better than sacrifice.

questions.prompt: Partial obedience is not the same as faithful obedience.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: Saul tried to explain disobedience as if it were worship.

questions.prompt: When God chose David, He showed that He sees...
choices: the heart, not only outward appearance
outward ability as the safest way to choose
appearance and experience above the heart
questions.correctAnswer: the heart, not only outward appearance
questions.reviewNote: God told Samuel that He looks on the heart.

questions.prompt: God promised David...
choices: a kingdom and throne connected to his family line
that David's family would never need correction
that a king could remove the need for repentance
questions.correctAnswer: a kingdom and throne connected to his family line
questions.reviewNote: The Davidic covenant points forward to Christ.

lessons.title: Solomon and the Temple
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise wisdom, temple worship, prayer, and the danger of divided loyalty.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise wisdom, temple worship, prayer, and the danger of divided loyalty.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Solomon and the Temple Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Solomon's wisdom, the temple, and why divided loyalty mattered.

steps.title: Solomon's Request
steps.prompt: Solomon asked God especially for...
choices: wisdom to govern God's people
success before wisdom
security without dependence on God
steps.correctAnswer: wisdom to govern God's people
steps.feedback: Correct. Solomon asked for an understanding heart to govern well.
steps.hint: Solomon asked for an understanding heart to govern well.

steps.title: True Wisdom
steps.prompt: Biblical wisdom begins with...
choices: reverence for the Lord
knowing enough to win arguments
having skill without the fear of the Lord
steps.correctAnswer: reverence for the Lord
steps.feedback: Correct. Wisdom is tied to fearing and honouring the Lord.
steps.hint: Wisdom is tied to fearing and honouring the Lord.

steps.title: Gift
steps.prompt: Solomon's wisdom was a gift from God.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God gave Solomon wisdom.
steps.hint: God gave Solomon wisdom.

steps.title: Temple Purpose
steps.prompt: The temple was built as...
choices: a place connected with God's name, worship, and prayer
a guarantee that worship would stay faithful
a place that made repentance less necessary
steps.correctAnswer: a place connected with God's name, worship, and prayer
steps.feedback: Correct. Solomon prayed that God would hear His people.
steps.hint: Solomon prayed that God would hear His people.

steps.title: Prayer for Nations
steps.prompt: Solomon's temple prayer included concern that...
choices: foreigners would know the Lord's name
foreigners could pray only after becoming Israelites first
the temple would make the nations more important than Israel
steps.correctAnswer: foreigners would know the Lord's name
steps.feedback: Correct. The prayer looked outward so the nations might know God.
steps.hint: The prayer looked outward so the nations might know God.

steps.title: Solomon's Fall
steps.prompt: Solomon's later idolatry showed the danger of...
choices: a divided heart
wisdom without the danger of compromise
temple worship without concern for daily obedience
steps.correctAnswer: a divided heart
steps.feedback: Correct. Solomon's heart turned away from the Lord.
steps.hint: Solomon's heart turned away from the Lord.

questions.prompt: Solomon asked God especially for...
choices: wisdom to govern God's people
success before wisdom
security without dependence on God
questions.correctAnswer: wisdom to govern God's people
questions.reviewNote: Solomon asked for an understanding heart to govern well.

questions.prompt: Biblical wisdom begins with...
choices: reverence for the Lord
knowing enough to win arguments
having skill without the fear of the Lord
questions.correctAnswer: reverence for the Lord
questions.reviewNote: Wisdom is tied to fearing and honouring the Lord.

questions.prompt: Solomon's wisdom was a gift from God.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: God gave Solomon wisdom.

questions.prompt: The temple was built as...
choices: a place connected with God's name, worship, and prayer
a guarantee that worship would stay faithful
a place that made repentance less necessary
questions.correctAnswer: a place connected with God's name, worship, and prayer
questions.reviewNote: Solomon prayed that God would hear His people.

questions.prompt: Solomon's temple prayer included concern that...
choices: foreigners would know the Lord's name
foreigners could pray only after becoming Israelites first
the temple would make the nations more important than Israel
questions.correctAnswer: foreigners would know the Lord's name
questions.reviewNote: The prayer looked outward so the nations might know God.

lessons.title: The Divided Kingdom
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise the divided kingdom, idolatry, warning, and God's patience.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise the divided kingdom, idolatry, warning, and God's patience.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: The Divided Kingdom Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand why the kingdom divided and why idolatry brought warning and judgment.

steps.title: After Solomon
steps.prompt: After Solomon, the kingdom divided into...
choices: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
Judah in the north and Israel in the south
one priestly kingdom and one royal kingdom
steps.correctAnswer: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
steps.feedback: Correct. The kingdom divided into northern Israel and southern Judah.
steps.hint: The kingdom divided into northern Israel and southern Judah.

steps.title: Rehoboam's Choice
steps.prompt: Rehoboam's harsh answer showed...
choices: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
strong leadership that listened after deciding
firmness that served the people well
steps.correctAnswer: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
steps.feedback: Correct. Rehoboam rejected wise counsel and answered harshly.
steps.hint: Rehoboam rejected wise counsel and answered harshly.

steps.title: Jeroboam's Calves
steps.prompt: Jeroboam set up golden calves because he...
choices: feared losing control of the people
wanted convenient worship without losing control
was trying to keep worship simple for the people
steps.correctAnswer: feared losing control of the people
steps.feedback: Correct. Jeroboam used false worship to protect his power.
steps.hint: Jeroboam used false worship to protect his power.

steps.title: False Worship
steps.prompt: Idolatry was a serious covenant sin, not a harmless worship style.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God had commanded His people to worship Him alone.
steps.hint: God had commanded His people to worship Him alone.

steps.title: God's Patience
steps.prompt: God sent prophets because...
choices: He warned His people and called them back
He cared about politics but not worship
He wanted sacrifice without covenant faithfulness
steps.correctAnswer: He warned His people and called them back
steps.feedback: Correct. The prophets showed God's patience and truth.
steps.hint: The prophets showed God's patience and truth.

steps.title: Exile Warning
steps.prompt: The fall of northern Israel showed that...
choices: God's warnings about idolatry were serious
religious identity could survive without repentance
national strength could protect false worship
steps.correctAnswer: God's warnings about idolatry were serious
steps.feedback: Correct. Judgment came after repeated warnings.
steps.hint: Judgment came after repeated warnings.

questions.prompt: After Solomon, the kingdom divided into...
choices: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
Judah in the north and Israel in the south
one priestly kingdom and one royal kingdom
questions.correctAnswer: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
questions.reviewNote: The kingdom divided into northern Israel and southern Judah.

questions.prompt: Rehoboam's harsh answer showed...
choices: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
strong leadership that listened after deciding
firmness that served the people well
questions.correctAnswer: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
questions.reviewNote: Rehoboam rejected wise counsel and answered harshly.

questions.prompt: Jeroboam set up golden calves because he...
choices: feared losing control of the people
wanted convenient worship without losing control
was trying to keep worship simple for the people
questions.correctAnswer: feared losing control of the people
questions.reviewNote: Jeroboam used false worship to protect his power.

questions.prompt: Idolatry was a serious covenant sin, not a harmless worship style.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: God had commanded His people to worship Him alone.

questions.prompt: God sent prophets because...
choices: He warned His people and called them back
He cared about politics but not worship
He wanted sacrifice without covenant faithfulness
questions.correctAnswer: He warned His people and called them back
questions.reviewNote: The prophets showed God's patience and truth.

lessons.title: Psalms and Worship
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise praise, lament, confession, thanksgiving, and trust in the Psalms.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise praise, lament, confession, thanksgiving, and trust in the Psalms.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Psalms and Worship Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how the Psalms teach God's people to pray, sing, confess, and trust.

steps.title: Praise
steps.prompt: A psalm of praise mainly...
choices: honours God for who He is and what He has done
uses correct words without personal trust
keeps worship separate from real life
steps.correctAnswer: honours God for who He is and what He has done
steps.feedback: Correct. Praise lifts attention to God's character and works.
steps.hint: Praise lifts attention to God's character and works.

steps.title: Lament
steps.prompt: A lament psalm helps God's people...
choices: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
hide pain so worship sounds stronger
turn pain into the center of worship
steps.correctAnswer: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
steps.feedback: Correct. Lament is honest prayer that still turns to God.
steps.hint: Lament is honest prayer that still turns to God.

steps.title: Psalm 51
steps.prompt: Psalm 51 teaches confession by...
choices: naming sin honestly before God
explaining sin until it sounds reasonable
confessing only the parts other people saw
steps.correctAnswer: naming sin honestly before God
steps.feedback: Correct. David confesses sin and asks for mercy.
steps.hint: David confesses sin and asks for mercy.

steps.title: Mercy
steps.prompt: The Psalms teach both honest confession and trust in God's mercy.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Biblical worship brings truth and mercy together.
steps.hint: Biblical worship brings truth and mercy together.

steps.title: Psalm 23
steps.prompt: Psalm 23 calls the Lord a shepherd to show...
choices: His care, guidance, and protection
that comfort is the main sign of faith
that fear disappears when faith is real
steps.correctAnswer: His care, guidance, and protection
steps.feedback: Correct. The shepherd picture teaches God's care even in hard places.
steps.hint: The shepherd picture teaches God's care even in hard places.

steps.title: Meditation
steps.prompt: The Psalms train God's people to...
choices: bring the whole heart under God's truth
use worship words without changed desires
make feelings the measure of truth
steps.correctAnswer: bring the whole heart under God's truth
steps.feedback: Correct. The Psalms teach prayer, worship, and trust with the whole heart.
steps.hint: The Psalms teach prayer, worship, and trust with the whole heart.

questions.prompt: A psalm of praise mainly...
choices: honours God for who He is and what He has done
uses correct words without personal trust
keeps worship separate from real life
questions.correctAnswer: honours God for who He is and what He has done
questions.reviewNote: Praise lifts attention to God's character and works.

questions.prompt: A lament psalm helps God's people...
choices: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
hide pain so worship sounds stronger
turn pain into the center of worship
questions.correctAnswer: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
questions.reviewNote: Lament is honest prayer that still turns to God.

questions.prompt: Psalm 51 teaches confession by...
choices: naming sin honestly before God
explaining sin until it sounds reasonable
confessing only the parts other people saw
questions.correctAnswer: naming sin honestly before God
questions.reviewNote: David confesses sin and asks for mercy.

questions.prompt: The Psalms teach both honest confession and trust in God's mercy.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: Biblical worship brings truth and mercy together.

questions.prompt: Psalm 23 calls the Lord a shepherd to show...
choices: His care, guidance, and protection
that comfort is the main sign of faith
that fear disappears when faith is real
questions.correctAnswer: His care, guidance, and protection
questions.reviewNote: The shepherd picture teaches God's care even in hard places.

lessons.title: Prophets Speak God's Word
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise prophetic warning, justice, mercy, hope, and the promised Saviour.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise prophetic warning, justice, mercy, hope, and the promised Saviour.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Prophets Speak God's Word Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how prophets warned, corrected, and gave hope from God.

steps.title: Speaking for God
steps.prompt: A true prophet spoke...
choices: God's message, not private opinion
God's word only when it sounded acceptable
religious speeches without covenant authority
steps.correctAnswer: God's message, not private opinion
steps.feedback: Correct. Prophets spoke the word of the Lord.
steps.hint: Prophets spoke the word of the Lord.

steps.title: Warning
steps.prompt: Prophets often warned God's people about...
choices: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
strict worship forms more than heart obedience
obedience that also cared about neighbours
steps.correctAnswer: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
steps.feedback: Correct. The prophets called people back to covenant faithfulness.
steps.hint: The prophets called people back to covenant faithfulness.

steps.title: Hope
steps.prompt: The prophets included hope as well as warning.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. They warned of judgment and also spoke God's promises.
steps.hint: They warned of judgment and also spoke God's promises.

steps.title: Micah 6:8
steps.prompt: Micah 6:8 calls God's people to...
choices: do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with God
do justice without humbly walking with God
love mercy while treating sin lightly
steps.correctAnswer: do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with God
steps.feedback: Correct. God cares about worship and daily life.
steps.hint: God cares about worship and daily life.

steps.title: New Covenant
steps.prompt: Jeremiah's new covenant promise pointed to...
choices: God writing His law on hearts and forgiving sin
God lowering His commands so people could keep them
a covenant that made outward religion enough
steps.correctAnswer: God writing His law on hearts and forgiving sin
steps.feedback: Correct. The new covenant promised inner renewal and forgiveness.
steps.hint: The new covenant promised inner renewal and forgiveness.

steps.title: Suffering Servant
steps.prompt: Isaiah 53 points to a servant who...
choices: suffers for the sins of others
serves without bearing guilt or sorrow
suffers only because he was weaker than his enemies
steps.correctAnswer: suffers for the sins of others
steps.feedback: Correct. Christians see Isaiah 53 fulfilled in Jesus Christ.
steps.hint: Christians see Isaiah 53 fulfilled in Jesus Christ.

questions.prompt: A true prophet spoke...
choices: God's message, not private opinion
God's word only when it sounded acceptable
religious speeches without covenant authority
questions.correctAnswer: God's message, not private opinion
questions.reviewNote: Prophets spoke the word of the Lord.

questions.prompt: Prophets often warned God's people about...
choices: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
strict worship forms more than heart obedience
obedience that also cared about neighbours
questions.correctAnswer: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
questions.reviewNote: The prophets called people back to covenant faithfulness.

questions.prompt: The prophets included hope as well as warning.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: They warned of judgment and also spoke God's promises.

questions.prompt: Micah 6:8 calls God's people to...
choices: do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with God
do justice without humbly walking with God
love mercy while treating sin lightly
questions.correctAnswer: do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with God
questions.reviewNote: God cares about worship and daily life.

questions.prompt: Jeremiah's new covenant promise pointed to...
choices: God writing His law on hearts and forgiving sin
God lowering His commands so people could keep them
a covenant that made outward religion enough
questions.correctAnswer: God writing His law on hearts and forgiving sin
questions.reviewNote: The new covenant promised inner renewal and forgiveness.

lessons.title: Exile
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise exile as judgment after warnings, while also seeing God's continuing promises.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise exile as judgment after warnings, while also seeing God's continuing promises.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Exile Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand exile as serious judgment and not the end of God's promises.

steps.title: Covenant Unfaithfulness
steps.prompt: Exile came because God's people repeatedly...
choices: rejected His covenant and ignored warnings
needed better strategy more than covenant faithfulness
were faithful but badly outnumbered
steps.correctAnswer: rejected His covenant and ignored warnings
steps.feedback: Correct. Exile followed long covenant unfaithfulness and warnings.
steps.hint: Exile followed long covenant unfaithfulness and warnings.

steps.title: God's Warnings
steps.prompt: God's warnings through prophets showed...
choices: patience before judgment
a warning system without real consequences
patience that did not care about repentance
steps.correctAnswer: patience before judgment
steps.feedback: Correct. God warned His people again and again.
steps.hint: God warned His people again and again.

steps.title: Serious Judgment
steps.prompt: Exile showed that idolatry and injustice were serious to God.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God's judgment treated covenant sin seriously.
steps.hint: God's judgment treated covenant sin seriously.

steps.title: Daniel's Faithfulness
steps.prompt: Daniel's early story shows that God's people could...
choices: remain faithful even away from the land
serve God only when worship was public
stay faithful by blending in quietly
steps.correctAnswer: remain faithful even away from the land
steps.feedback: Correct. Daniel trusted God while living in Babylon.
steps.hint: Daniel trusted God while living in Babylon.

steps.title: God Still Rules
steps.prompt: Exile did not mean that...
choices: God had lost rule over the nations
God had warned His people for many years
God was still ruling outside the land
steps.correctAnswer: God had lost rule over the nations
steps.feedback: Correct. God still ruled even over Babylon.
steps.hint: God still ruled even over Babylon.

steps.title: Hope in Exile
steps.prompt: Even in exile, God's people could hope because...
choices: God's covenant promises had not failed
time away from the land would heal sin by itself
a new empire would become their true hope
steps.correctAnswer: God's covenant promises had not failed
steps.feedback: Correct. God's promises continued even through judgment.
steps.hint: God's promises continued even through judgment.

questions.prompt: Exile came because God's people repeatedly...
choices: rejected His covenant and ignored warnings
needed better strategy more than covenant faithfulness
were faithful but badly outnumbered
questions.correctAnswer: rejected His covenant and ignored warnings
questions.reviewNote: Exile followed long covenant unfaithfulness and warnings.

questions.prompt: God's warnings through prophets showed...
choices: patience before judgment
a warning system without real consequences
patience that did not care about repentance
questions.correctAnswer: patience before judgment
questions.reviewNote: God warned His people again and again.

questions.prompt: Exile showed that idolatry and injustice were serious to God.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: God's judgment treated covenant sin seriously.

questions.prompt: Daniel's early story shows that God's people could...
choices: remain faithful even away from the land
serve God only when worship was public
stay faithful by blending in quietly
questions.correctAnswer: remain faithful even away from the land
questions.reviewNote: Daniel trusted God while living in Babylon.

questions.prompt: Exile did not mean that...
choices: God had lost rule over the nations
God had warned His people for many years
God was still ruling outside the land
questions.correctAnswer: God had lost rule over the nations
questions.reviewNote: God still ruled even over Babylon.

lessons.title: Return and Waiting
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise return from exile, rebuilding, worship restored, and waiting for Christ.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise return from exile, rebuilding, worship restored, and waiting for Christ.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Return and Waiting Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand the return from exile and why God's people were still waiting for the promised Saviour.

steps.title: Cyrus' Decree
steps.prompt: The return from exile began when God moved...
choices: Cyrus to allow the Jews to return
a later Egyptian king to help Judah return
a new king from David's line to rebuild immediately
steps.correctAnswer: Cyrus to allow the Jews to return
steps.feedback: Correct. God ruled even through a Persian king.
steps.hint: God ruled even through a Persian king.

steps.title: Temple Rebuilt
steps.prompt: Rebuilding the temple showed that...
choices: worship was being restored
the temple building itself would fix their hearts
returning home meant obedience would now be easy
steps.correctAnswer: worship was being restored
steps.feedback: Correct. The returned people restored temple worship.
steps.hint: The returned people restored temple worship.

steps.title: God's Faithfulness
steps.prompt: The return from exile showed God's faithfulness to His promises.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God brought His people back as He had promised.
steps.hint: God brought His people back as He had promised.

steps.title: Nehemiah
steps.prompt: Nehemiah's rebuilding work showed...
choices: prayer, courage, and practical leadership
planning that made prayer unnecessary
public success mattered more than faithfulness
steps.correctAnswer: prayer, courage, and practical leadership
steps.feedback: Correct. Nehemiah prayed and worked in dependence on God.
steps.hint: Nehemiah prayed and worked in dependence on God.

steps.title: Opposition
steps.prompt: Opposition to rebuilding taught God's people to...
choices: persevere while trusting God
wait until opponents respected their work
measure faithfulness by how safe the work felt
steps.correctAnswer: persevere while trusting God
steps.feedback: Correct. Faithfulness often requires courage under pressure.
steps.hint: Faithfulness often requires courage under pressure.

steps.title: Not the Full Ending
steps.prompt: After the return, God's people were still waiting for...
choices: the promised Saviour and full restoration
political comfort as the final promise
a leader who would only restore their walls
steps.correctAnswer: the promised Saviour and full restoration
steps.feedback: Correct. The Old Testament ends with hope still looking forward.
steps.hint: The Old Testament ends with hope still looking forward.

questions.prompt: The return from exile began when God moved...
choices: Cyrus to allow the Jews to return
a later Egyptian king to help Judah return
a new king from David's line to rebuild immediately
questions.correctAnswer: Cyrus to allow the Jews to return
questions.reviewNote: God ruled even through a Persian king.

questions.prompt: Rebuilding the temple showed that...
choices: worship was being restored
the temple building itself would fix their hearts
returning home meant obedience would now be easy
questions.correctAnswer: worship was being restored
questions.reviewNote: The returned people restored temple worship.

questions.prompt: The return from exile showed God's faithfulness to His promises.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: God brought His people back as He had promised.

questions.prompt: Nehemiah's rebuilding work showed...
choices: prayer, courage, and practical leadership
planning that made prayer unnecessary
public success mattered more than faithfulness
questions.correctAnswer: prayer, courage, and practical leadership
questions.reviewNote: Nehemiah prayed and worked in dependence on God.

questions.prompt: Opposition to rebuilding taught God's people to...
choices: persevere while trusting God
wait until opponents respected their work
measure faithfulness by how safe the work felt
questions.correctAnswer: persevere while trusting God
questions.reviewNote: Faithfulness often requires courage under pressure.

lessons.title: Kingdom, Psalms, Prophets, Exile, and Return Final Quiz
lessons.teacherSummary: Students complete a scored review quiz for Kingdom, Psalms, Prophets, Exile, and Return.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students complete a scored review quiz for Kingdom, Psalms, Prophets, Exile, and Return.

quiz.title: Kingdom, Psalms, Prophets, Exile, and Return: Final Quiz

questions.section: Judges and the Need for a King
questions.prompt: The book of Judges often shows a cycle of...
choices: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
short-term rescue followed by lasting faithfulness
prophets, temple worship, and rebuilding
questions.correctAnswer: sin, trouble, crying out, and rescue
questions.reviewNote: Israel repeatedly turned from God and needed rescue.

questions.section: Judges and the Need for a King
questions.prompt: Israel's worship of other gods showed...
choices: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
a practical way to fit in with nearby cultures
a small compromise to keep peace with neighbours
questions.correctAnswer: unfaithfulness to the covenant Lord
questions.reviewNote: Idolatry broke covenant faithfulness.

questions.section: Judges and the Need for a King
questions.prompt: God raised up judges as acts of mercy toward His oppressed people.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: The judges were imperfect rescuers, but God's mercy was real.

questions.prompt: Samuel's call showed that Israel needed...
choices: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
leaders who kept peace by softening hard messages
a prophet who spoke only when people agreed
questions.correctAnswer: God's word faithfully heard and spoken
questions.reviewNote: Samuel learned to listen and speak God's word.

questions.prompt: Saul's failure showed that God desires...
choices: obedience more than outward religious action
public worship that covers private rebellion
leaders who protect reputation before obedience
questions.correctAnswer: obedience more than outward religious action
questions.reviewNote: Samuel told Saul that obedience is better than sacrifice.

questions.prompt: Partial obedience is not the same as faithful obedience.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: Saul tried to explain disobedience as if it were worship.

questions.prompt: Solomon asked God especially for...
choices: wisdom to govern God's people
success before wisdom
security without dependence on God
questions.correctAnswer: wisdom to govern God's people
questions.reviewNote: Solomon asked for an understanding heart to govern well.

questions.prompt: Biblical wisdom begins with...
choices: reverence for the Lord
knowing enough to win arguments
having skill without the fear of the Lord
questions.correctAnswer: reverence for the Lord
questions.reviewNote: Wisdom is tied to fearing and honouring the Lord.

questions.prompt: Solomon's wisdom was a gift from God.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: God gave Solomon wisdom.

questions.prompt: After Solomon, the kingdom divided into...
choices: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
Judah in the north and Israel in the south
one priestly kingdom and one royal kingdom
questions.correctAnswer: Israel in the north and Judah in the south
questions.reviewNote: The kingdom divided into northern Israel and southern Judah.

questions.prompt: Rehoboam's harsh answer showed...
choices: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
strong leadership that listened after deciding
firmness that served the people well
questions.correctAnswer: foolish leadership that helped divide the kingdom
questions.reviewNote: Rehoboam rejected wise counsel and answered harshly.

questions.prompt: Jeroboam set up golden calves because he...
choices: feared losing control of the people
wanted convenient worship without losing control
was trying to keep worship simple for the people
questions.correctAnswer: feared losing control of the people
questions.reviewNote: Jeroboam used false worship to protect his power.

questions.prompt: A psalm of praise mainly...
choices: honours God for who He is and what He has done
uses correct words without personal trust
keeps worship separate from real life
questions.correctAnswer: honours God for who He is and what He has done
questions.reviewNote: Praise lifts attention to God's character and works.

questions.prompt: A lament psalm helps God's people...
choices: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
hide pain so worship sounds stronger
turn pain into the center of worship
questions.correctAnswer: bring sorrow and trouble to God in faith
questions.reviewNote: Lament is honest prayer that still turns to God.

questions.prompt: Psalm 51 teaches confession by...
choices: naming sin honestly before God
explaining sin until it sounds reasonable
confessing only the parts other people saw
questions.correctAnswer: naming sin honestly before God
questions.reviewNote: David confesses sin and asks for mercy.

questions.prompt: A true prophet spoke...
choices: God's message, not private opinion
God's word only when it sounded acceptable
religious speeches without covenant authority
questions.correctAnswer: God's message, not private opinion
questions.reviewNote: Prophets spoke the word of the Lord.

questions.prompt: Prophets often warned God's people about...
choices: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
strict worship forms more than heart obedience
obedience that also cared about neighbours
questions.correctAnswer: idolatry, injustice, and empty worship
questions.reviewNote: The prophets called people back to covenant faithfulness.

questions.prompt: The prophets included hope as well as warning.
choices: True
False
questions.correctAnswer: True
questions.reviewNote: They warned of judgment and also spoke God's promises.
